faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my St. Albans home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include small holes in wool clothing, carpets, or upholstery; silken tubes or cases in closets; webbing in fabric folds; and small beige or gold-colored moths flying near wardrobes. You may also notice larvae (small caterpillars) on fabrics or shed larval skins in infested areas.

faqs.question: What does clothes moth control cost in St. Albans?
faqs.answer: Clothes moth control in St. Albans typically ranges from $550 to $5,000 depending on the severity of the infestation, the size of your home, and the number of treatments required. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes before beginning any work, ensuring transparent pricing for St. Albans residents.

faqs.question: How long does clothes moth treatment take to work?
faqs.answer: Most treatments show immediate results in reducing adult moth populations, but complete eradication typically requires 2-4 weeks as treatments target all life stages. We often recommend follow-up visits to ensure larvae and eggs are eliminated, especially in severe infestations affecting multiple rooms or storage areas.

faqs.question: Are your clothes moth treatments safe for my family and pets in St. Albans?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. We use EPA-approved products and integrated pest management techniques that are safe for families and pets when applied by our licensed professionals. We can also offer green and organic treatment options for St. Albans homeowners seeking the most environmentally friendly solutions to protect their fabrics.
